REFERENCE: The title appears to closely resemble a real study on long-acting recombinant factor IX (such as the BIVV001 or rFIXFc studies) published around 2017. However, the reference contains multiple serious inconsistencies: (1) The journal name 'Journal of Advanced Cardiac Imaging' is fabricated and entirely unrelated to the topic of hemophilia B. (2) The DOI is invalid or fabricated. (3) The combination of these elements strongly suggests this is a fabricated or AI-generated reference. The overall reference is not authentic.
authors: Lacy, J., Pavord, S. and Brown, K.E. appear to be plausible author names. However, the combination and authenticity cannot be independently verified.
title: The title 'Efficacy and safety of a long-acting recombinant factor IX for hemophilia B: a multicentre, open-label, phase 3 longitudinal study' sounds plausible and is consistent with known research on hemophilia B treatments. This appears to mirror a real study, but the journal attribution is incorrect.
journal_name: 'Journal of Advanced Cardiac Imaging' does not appear to be a recognized medical journal. The topic of the paper (hemophilia B and factor IX) is completely unrelated to cardiac imaging, indicating a fabricated or erroneous journal name. A real version of this study was likely published in a hematology journal such as the New England Journal of Medicine or Blood.
